initial_description:
The diagram illustrates the layered architecture of CV Assistant, flowing from left to right. The background is a very light pastel gray.

**Web/CLI Layer (Left)**: This is the first major section, enclosed in a rounded rectangle with a thin, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white. Inside this rectangle, there are two components arranged vertically.

*   **Web App (FastAPI)**: A smaller rectangle labeled "Web App (FastAPI, port 8420)". The rectangle has a light blue fill. Below it is an icon representing a web browser. A WebSocket icon (a stylized "W" enclosed in a circle) connects it to the Agent Core.
*   **CLI (cv-agent)**: A smaller rectangle labeled "CLI (cv-agent)". The rectangle has a light green fill. Below it is an icon representing a command-line interface (a black rectangle with a white ">_" prompt). A WebSocket icon connects it to the Agent Core.

**Agent Core (Center)**: This is the second major section, enclosed in a larger rounded rectangle with a thicker, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white.

*   **LangGraph ReAct Orchestrator**: A central box labeled "LangGraph ReAct Orchestrator (agent.py)". It has a soft yellow fill. Below the label, it says "powered by a local Ollama LLM" in smaller font. Arrows connect this box to the Web/CLI layer (via WebSocket) and the Tool Layer.
*   **ZeroClaw Tool-Execution Layer**: A smaller box below the Orchestrator box, labeled "ZeroClaw tool-execution layer". It has a light orange fill and the text "wraps Python @tool functions" in smaller font.

**Tool Layer (Middle-Right)**: This section is represented by a large, horizontally oriented rectangle with a dashed dark gray border to indicate its modular nature. The fill color is white. Inside, 6 groups of tools are shown as vertically stacked boxes, each with a distinct color.

*   **Research Tools (light purple)**: Box labeled "Research tools". Contains comma-separated tool names: "search_arxiv, fetch_paper, extract_equations, generate_spec".
*   **Vision Tools (light teal)**: Box labeled "Vision tools". Contains comma-separated tool names: "analyze_image, describe_image". Below, "(Ollama VLM + MLX Apple Silicon)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **Knowledge Tools (light pink)**: Box labeled "Knowledge tools". Contains comma-separated tool names: "add_to_graph, query_graph". Below, "(Obsidian vault)" is written in smaller font.
*   **Utility Tools (light brown)**: Box labeled "Utility tools". Contains comma-separated tool names: "shell, file_read, file_write, web_search".
*   **Model Tools (light cyan)**: Box labeled "Model tools". Contains comma-separated tool names: "pull_vision_model, probe_hardware". Below, "(llmfit hardware detection)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **OCR Tools (pale yellow)**: Box labeled "OCR tools". Contains "run_ocr". Below, "(PaddleOCR, 80+ languages)" is written in a smaller font.

An arrow connects the ZeroClaw layer to the Tool Layer rectangle.

**Backend Layer (Right)**: The final section is enclosed in a rounded rectangle with a thin, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white. Inside, components are arranged vertically.

*   **Ollama**: A box labeled "Ollama". Below, "local LLM/VLM inference (qwen2.5-coder, qwen3-vl)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **MLX**: A box labeled "MLX". Below, "Apple Silicon neural-engine acceleration for SAM3 segmentation" is written in a smaller font.
*   **HuggingFace Hub**: A box labeled "HuggingFace Hub". Below, "model weight downloads (SAM 2/3, SD-Turbo, SDXL, DeepGen)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **ArXiv / Semantic Scholar**: A box labeled "ArXiv / Semantic Scholar". Below, "paper discovery and metadata" is written in a smaller font.
*   **Local filesystem + Obsidian vault**: A box labeled "Local filesystem + Obsidian vault". Below, "knowledge persistence" is written in a smaller font.

An arrow connects the Tool Layer to the Backend Layer rectangle.

Arrows indicate the flow of data/control from left to right, connecting the Web/CLI Layer to the Agent Core, the Agent Core to the Tool Layer, and the Tool Layer to the Backend Layer. All arrows are solid, dark gray, and of medium thickness.
optimized_description:
The diagram illustrates the layered architecture of CV Assistant, flowing from left to right. The background is a very light cream color.

**Web/CLI Layer (Left)**: This is the first major section, enclosed in a rounded rectangle with a thin, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white. Inside this rectangle, there are two components arranged vertically.

*   **Web App (FastAPI)**: A smaller rounded rectangle labeled "Web App (FastAPI, port 8420)". The rectangle has a soft sky blue fill. Below it is a simple icon representing a web browser. A WebSocket icon (a stylized "W" enclosed in a circle) connects it to the Agent Core, using a gently curved arrow.
*   **CLI (cv-agent)**: A smaller rounded rectangle labeled "CLI (cv-agent)". The rectangle has a light sage green fill. Below it is a simple icon representing a command-line interface (a black rectangle with a white ">_" prompt). A WebSocket icon connects it to the Agent Core, using a gently curved arrow.

**Agent Core (Center)**: This is the second major section, enclosed in a larger rounded rectangle with a thicker, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white.

*   **LangGraph ReAct Orchestrator**: A central rounded box labeled "LangGraph ReAct Orchestrator (agent.py)" in bold sans-serif font. It has a warm peach fill. Below the label, it says "powered by a local Ollama LLM" in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Arrows connect this box to the Web/CLI layer (via WebSocket) and the Tool Layer.
*   **ZeroClaw Tool-Execution Layer**: A smaller rounded box below the Orchestrator box, labeled "ZeroClaw tool-execution layer" in bold sans-serif font. It has a light orange fill and the text "wraps Python @tool functions" in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.

**Tool Layer (Middle-Right)**: This section is represented by a large, horizontally oriented rounded rectangle with a dashed dark gray border to indicate its modular nature. The fill color is white. Inside, 6 groups of tools are shown as vertically stacked rounded boxes, each with a distinct pastel color.

*   **Research Tools**: Rounded box labeled "Research tools" in bold sans-serif font with a light lavender fill. Contains comma-separated tool names: "search_arxiv, fetch_paper, extract_equations, generate_spec" in regular sans-serif font.
*   **Vision Tools**: Rounded box labeled "Vision tools" in bold sans-serif font with a light teal fill. Contains comma-separated tool names: "analyze_image, describe_image" in regular sans-serif font. Below, "(Ollama VLM + MLX Apple Silicon)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **Knowledge Tools**: Rounded box labeled "Knowledge tools" in bold sans-serif font with a light pink fill. Contains comma-separated tool names: "add_to_graph, query_graph" in regular sans-serif font. Below, "(Obsidian vault)" is written in smaller font.
*   **Utility Tools**: Rounded box labeled "Utility tools" in bold sans-serif font with a light tan fill. Contains comma-separated tool names: "shell, file_read, file_write, web_search" in regular sans-serif font.
*   **Model Tools**: Rounded box labeled "Model tools" in bold sans-serif font with a light cyan fill. Contains comma-separated tool names: "pull_vision_model, probe_hardware" in regular sans-serif font. Below, "(llmfit hardware detection)" is written in a smaller font.
*   **OCR Tools**: Rounded box labeled "OCR tools" in bold sans-serif font with a pale yellow fill. Contains "run_ocr" in regular sans-serif font. Below, "(PaddleOCR, 80+ languages)" is written in a smaller font.

An arrow connects the ZeroClaw layer to the Tool Layer rectangle.

**Backend Layer (Right)**: The final section is enclosed in a rounded rectangle with a thin, solid dark gray border. The fill color is white. Inside, components are arranged vertically.

*   **Ollama**: A rounded box labeled "Ollama" in bold sans-serif font. Below, "local LLM/VLM inference (qwen2.5-coder, qwen3-vl)" is written in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.
*   **MLX**: A rounded box labeled "MLX" in bold sans-serif font. Below, "Apple Silicon neural-engine acceleration for SAM3 segmentation" is written in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.
*   **HuggingFace Hub**: A rounded box labeled "HuggingFace Hub" in bold sans-serif font. Below, "model weight downloads (SAM 2/3, SD-Turbo, SDXL, DeepGen)" is written in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.
*   **ArXiv / Semantic Scholar**: A rounded box labeled "ArXiv / Semantic Scholar" in bold sans-serif font. Below, "paper discovery and metadata" is written in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.
*   **Local filesystem + Obsidian vault**: A rounded box labeled "Local filesystem + Obsidian vault" in bold sans-serif font. Below, "knowledge persistence" is written in a smaller, regular sans-serif font.

An arrow connects the Tool Layer to the Backend Layer rectangle.

Arrows indicate the flow of data/control from left to right, connecting the Web/CLI Layer to the Agent Core, the Agent Core to the Tool Layer, and the Tool Layer to the Backend Layer. All arrows are solid, dark gray, and of medium thickness. Use bezier curves for the arrows to soften the diagram.
